gene_region.label: WRN transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical WRN genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: WRN encodes the Werner syndrome RecQ helicase with exonuclease activity in genome maintenance. This topic bundle emphasizes aging and cellular maintenance, with interpretation centered on telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local WRN bundle is oriented toward aging and cellular maintenance. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: WRN variants can alter, tag, or contextualize telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Aging, longevity, and cellular maintenance research context
Telomere biology, DNA repair, oxidative stress, and senescence pathways
Progeroid, degenerative, cancer-risk, and stress-resilience review
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: WRN methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for WRN.
Because WRN biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use WRN promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
Aging, longevity, and cellular maintenance research context
Telomere biology, DNA repair, oxidative stress, and senescence pathways

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000165392: WRN coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: WRN biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: WRN: aging and cellular maintenance via telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a WRN aging and cellular maintenance thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_records.common_name: WRN aging and cellular maintenance pathway model
variant_records.clinical_interpretation: WRN aging-maintenance variation model is curated as a WRN marker for Aging, longevity, and cellular maintenance research context, Telomere biology, DNA repair, oxidative stress, and senescence pathways. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag telomere and genome maintenance, stress resilience, NAD-dependent signaling, proteostasis, oxidative response, and senescence biology.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
associated_conditions: Aging, longevity, and cellular maintenance research context
Telomere biology, DNA repair, oxidative stress, and senescence pathways
Progeroid, degenerative, cancer-risk, and stress-resilience review
research_context: Use this marker as a WRN pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: WRN aging and cellular maintenance pathway model
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair WRN aging-maintenance variation model with WRN methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.
